reference_scope: amule-official; emule-official-0.50a; mldonkey-official; wireshark-official; protocol-docs
current_surface: Historical tracker mentions older deleted references.
target_or_prune_reason: Use only the current authoritative local references. Treat the historical ED2K tracker as background, not authority. The RA2 reference map in overview.md records the concrete authoritative entry points.
user_impact: Future ED2K work stops depending on stale reference assumptions.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule/MLDonkey session and socket ownership
current_surface: aria2-next RequestGroup, DownloadEngine, Command/EventPoll, Ed2kAttribute, Ed2kCommand, Ed2kKadCommand
target_or_prune_reason: Keep aria2-next lifecycle ownership. Current source inventory is mapped in overview.md. Ed2kCommand remains the overloaded TCP owner for server and peer sessions. Ed2kKadCommand remains the single UDP owner for both ED2K server UDP and Kad. Split only when a later checkpoint proves a correctness, capability-truth, or maintainability need.
user_impact: No sidecar runtime or second event loop is introduced.

reference_scope: aMule ED2KLink; eMule ED2KLink; protocol-docs link grammar
target_or_prune_reason: Aligned file, server, serverlist, nodeslist, and search link parsing. File links preserve MD4 root hashes, part hashes, AICH roots, inline sources, source hashes, and crypt options. Path separators in file names are normalized before aria2-next output path ownership. File size validation follows the 2^38 byte eMule/aMule limit. Search links are parsed as metadata and not treated as normal downloads.
user_impact: Users can paste common ED2K links without unsafe filenames or lost metadata.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule ServerList and Server; MLDonkey server state; Wireshark tags
current_surface: ed2k_server.*; Ed2kAttribute serverStates; session hidden state
target_or_prune_reason: Align server.met parsing, metadata tags, duplicate merge, malformed input handling, TCP/UDP capability state, retry, and persistence.
user_impact: Server discovery state survives restart and remains accurate.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule Kad contacts; MLDonkey nodes.dat; protocol docs
current_surface: ed2k_kad.*; Ed2kKadState; session hidden Kad state
target_or_prune_reason: Align nodes.dat parsing, contact validation, routing snapshot, bootstrap merge, and durable state. Prune obsolete Kad1-only behavior.
user_impact: Kad bootstrap remains native and restart-safe.

reference_scope: eMule/aMule part.met; known.met; known2.met; clients.met; MLDonkey state
current_surface: aria2-next control files, session serializer, SharedStore, UploadQueue
target_or_prune_reason: Replace legacy databases with aria2-next control/session/shared-store formats. Import legacy formats only if a concrete migration requirement is later approved.
user_impact: No second ED2K database stack is introduced.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule ServerSocket; MLDonkey server protocol; Wireshark ED2K dissector; protocol docs
target_or_prune_reason: Align login, IDChange, HighID/LowID, GetSources, GetSources OBFU, FoundSources, FoundSources OBFU, callback, status, messages, server list, search transport, throttling, and persistence.
user_impact: Server source discovery handles modern and older server formats.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule ServerUDPSocket; MLDonkey UDP behavior; Wireshark ED2K UDP
current_surface: Ed2kKadCommand ED2K UDP path; ed2k_server.*; ed2k_peer.*
target_or_prune_reason: Align global status, global sources, packed replies, large-file UDP forms, callback fail, retry, and state updates while keeping one UDP command path.
user_impact: UDP improves discovery without a second subsystem.

reference_scope: protocol-docs callback; aMule/eMule callback handling; MLDonkey indirect addresses
target_or_prune_reason: Align direct HighID paths, server-mediated LowID callback, LowID-to-LowID impossible states, callback fail, timeout, and safe reporting. Prune buddy-only callback unless required by non-pruned transfer paths.
user_impact: LowID behavior becomes deterministic and honest.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule ClientTCPSocket and ListenSocket; Wireshark eMule dissector
target_or_prune_reason: Align hello, hello answer, eMule info, misc options, misc options 2, server endpoint fields, and parse tolerance.
user_impact: Peers see accurate client identity and compatible handshake packets.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule misc options; MLDonkey defaults; Wireshark tags
target_or_prune_reason: Advertise only capabilities with implemented send, receive, state, error, and fallback paths. Safely parse unsupported remote claims without mirroring them.
user_impact: Modern peers do not select packet flows aria2-next cannot complete.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule encrypted sockets and obfuscation metadata; Wireshark crypt fields
current_surface: Source crypt metadata exists; no complete encrypted transport owner is evident
target_or_prune_reason: Parse and preserve source/server crypt metadata. Implement encrypted transport only if required and feasible inside existing socket ownership; otherwise keep crypt unadvertised and skip require-crypt sources.
user_impact: Avoids failed encrypted paths caused by false capability claims.

reference_scope: eMule secure identification; aMule credits; Wireshark SEC_IDENT/PUBLIC_KEY/SIGNATURE
current_surface: Upload credit counters exist; secure-ident flow is incomplete
target_or_prune_reason: Implement secure-ident only if needed for non-pruned interoperability. Otherwise keep it unadvertised and document credit-security limitations.
user_impact: Upload credits stay honest without claiming identity verification.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule DownloadClient and ClientTCPSocket; protocol docs; Wireshark multipacket
target_or_prune_reason: Align request filename variants, file status, hashset, start upload, queue rank, accept upload, AICH request, source request, failure packets, multipacket, extended multipacket, and file identifiers.
user_impact: Peer sessions can reach queue or transfer state with modern clients.

reference_scope: eMule FileIdentifier; aMule KnownFile; protocol docs
target_or_prune_reason: Represent file identity as ED2K root hash, size, optional MD4 hashset, optional AICH root, and compatible comparison rules using aria2-next metadata ownership.
user_impact: Large-file and AICH-aware peers can validate file identity.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule transfer states; protocol docs
target_or_prune_reason: Align request parts, I64 parts, sending parts, compressed parts, out-of-parts, cancel, no-file, disconnect, duplicate rejection, retry scoring, and backoff.
user_impact: Failed peers recover cleanly and valid peers can transfer safely.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule PartFile/KnownFile/AICH; protocol-docs corruption handling
target_or_prune_reason: Align normal/I64/compressed data, range validation, MD4 part/root/final hashes, AICH root/tree/recovery, corrupt retry, and resume safety through existing disk paths.
user_impact: Downloaded data is verified and restart-safe.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule source management and DeadSourceList; MLDonkey source handling
target_or_prune_reason: Align source priority, server/OBFU/inline/resume/SX/Kad sources, callback constraints, queue rank, availability, dead-source backoff, duplicate rejection, and endgame behavior.
user_impact: Downloads avoid tight loops and unsafe duplicate writes.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule Source Exchange; Wireshark source packets
target_or_prune_reason: Align SX1, SX2, SX2 v4, user hash, server endpoint, crypt options, direct callback metadata where supported, deduplication, loopback/self filtering, and backoff preservation.
user_impact: Peers can discover extra sources safely.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule Kademlia; MLDonkey Kad; protocol docs; Kademlia paper
target_or_prune_reason: Align classic Kad nodes.dat, bootstrap, hello, routing, traversal, source search, source publish, keyword search, firewalled checks, refresh, and durable state. Prune obsolete Kad1/UI behavior.
user_impact: Kad discovery remains native and maintainable.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule SharedFileList/KnownFile; MLDonkey share model
target_or_prune_reason: Align completed-file sharing, imported file hashing, known-file metadata, duplicate handling, file status, hashset, AICH, Source Exchange answer, and part serving.
user_impact: aria2-next can behave as a cooperative file source where enabled.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule UploadQueue/UploadClient/ClientCredits; protocol docs
target_or_prune_reason: Align queue admission, queue rank, slot limits, uploaded/downloaded counters, waiting state, UDP reask rank, and credit behavior. Prune friend slots and UI-only controls.
user_impact: Peers see valid upload queue behavior without legacy UI policy.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule SearchList/SearchExpr; MLDonkey search; protocol docs
target_or_prune_reason: Align server and Kad search, expression terms, typed tags, result merge, source counts, complete source counts, file type, extension, media metadata, source networks, more-results, and generated ED2K links.
user_impact: Motrix Next can search and start normal ED2K tasks.

reference_scope: aria2-next RPC patterns; Motrix integration needs; legacy DTOs only as contrast
target_or_prune_reason: Expose stable ED2K task/search/share/upload/Kad/server/peer fields only when they reflect implemented behavior.
user_impact: Integrators receive accurate status without unsupported claims.

reference_scope: aMule GUI/web/text client; eMule Windows UI; MLDonkey UI surfaces
target_or_prune_reason: Prune GUI-only, daemon-shell, web UI, telnet UI, remote GUI, chat, preview, collection, captcha, rating, and shared-directory browsing surfaces.
user_impact: No legacy UI compatibility is promised.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule UPnP/NAT helpers; MLDonkey port helpers
current_surface: aria2-next listen-port options and existing networking
target_or_prune_reason: Replace legacy UPnP/NAT-PMP/router-test stacks with explicit aria2-next listen-port configuration unless a native owner is later approved.
user_impact: No extra NAT dependency is introduced.
